query: What is the current status of SpaceX Starship Flight 13 (IFT-13) as of mid-July 2026? Has Booster 20 completed its static fire test? Has the FAA approved Flight 13 / accepted the Flight 12 mishap investigation? What is the latest NET (no earlier than) launch date shown on trackers like NextSpaceflight and RGV Aerial Photography?
answer:
I've completed my research on SpaceX Starship Flight 13 (IFT-13). Here's the bottom line:

**Booster 20 static fire:** ✅ COMPLETED — a full-duration static fire firing all 33 Raptor 3 engines on **July 10, 2026**, described as the last major pre-flight test.

**Launch NET:** ~**July 16, 2026** (90-minute window opening 6:45 PM ET / 10:45 PM UTC from Starbase Pad 2). SpaceX announced this July 11; an earlier July 15 target slipped ~1 day. NextSpaceflight showed 14/20 checklist items complete as of July 13. Vehicles: **Ship 40 + Booster 20** (V3/Block 3).

**FAA / Flight 12 mishap investigation:** Flight 12 (May 22, Booster 19 + Ship 39) suffered a booster failure; the FAA declared a mishap and required an investigation on May 27, grounding Starship. I could **not find an explicit FAA press release formally closing the Flight 12 investigation or granting the Flight 13 license** in the available sources. However, the fact that SpaceX has publicly announced a firm launch window, completed the final static fire, and is proceeding toward stacking strongly implies the regulatory path is effectively cleared or imminent — SpaceX does not announce firm dates without the license modification in hand. This is the one point of residual uncertainty and is flagged as such in my findings.

sources.title: SpaceX gears up for Starship Flight 13
sources.snippet: SpaceX plans to conduct its next Starship launch as soon as July 16... targeting a 90-minute window that opens at 6:45 p.m. Eastern... a day after completing a static fire of the Super Heavy booster.

sources.title: SpaceX ignites all 33 powerful engines on Starship booster ahead of Flight 13 launch
sources.snippet: SpaceX today (July 10) completed a brief static fire of the Starship Super Heavy booster... Booster 20... all 33 engines ignited.

sources.title: Starship Flight 13 Targets July 15 After Booster 20 Clears Its Static Fire
sources.snippet: SpaceX completed a full-duration, 33-engine static fire of Super Heavy Booster 20 on Friday morning — the last major pre-flight test... targeting a launch window opening Wednesday, July 15, at 6:45 PM ET from Pad 2.

sources.title: SpaceX Super Heavy V3 Static Fire: Flight 13 Q&A
sources.snippet: UPDATE July 12, 2026: Musk confirmed on X the next launch targeting Thursday; Flight 13 now targeting NET Thursday, July 16th at 5:45 PM CDT, a slight slip from the previously reported July 14-15 window.

sources.title: Starship | Next Spaceflight
sources.snippet: July 13... 14/20 Checklist Items Complete. Booster 20 Static Fire Campaign, Ship 40 Static Fire Campaign, Raptor 3 Engines Installed, vehicles at launch site.

sources.title: FAA requires SpaceX-led mishap investigation before resumption of Starship launches
sources.snippet: FAA determination after analyzing Flight 12 (May 22); SpaceX must complete a mishap investigation into the 12th flight before it can launch again.

sources.title: FAA requires mishap investigation into latest Starship launch
sources.snippet: In a May 27 statement, the FAA said the Flight 12 launch of Starship on May 22 resulted in a mishap.

sources.title: SpaceX Starship Grounded After Flight 12 Booster Failure: FAA Investigation
sources.snippet: FAA formally declared the May 22 Starship Flight 12 launch a mishap and ordered a full investigation before Flight 13 can proceed; Raptor 3 engines failed to reignite, sending Booster 19 to a hard splashdown.

sources.title: Starship flight test 12
sources.snippet: Flight 12 used Booster 19 and Ship 39, first use of Block 3 vehicles, first launch from Starbase's second launch pad.

sources.title: SpaceX Fires All 33 Raptor Engines as Starship Moves One Step Closer to Its Next Launch
sources.snippet: Successfully igniting all 33 Raptor engines on Super Heavy during a full-duration static fire at Starbase, Texas.

sources.title: Starship Flight 13 Mission - RocketLaunch.Live
sources.snippet: Launch Time 10:45 PM UTC listed for Flight 13.
